entries.statement: Knowledge of statistical foundations, including descriptive statistics, inferential statistics, probability theory, distributions, hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, and the distinction between statistical significance and practical significance.

Knowledge of sampling methodology, including probability and non-probability sampling designs, sample size determination, margin of error calculation, representativeness assessment, and the impact of sampling choices on analytical validity.

Knowledge of mathematical modeling and quantitative methods, including applied mathematics, model design, parameter estimation, model validation, and the selection of appropriate quantitative approaches for business problems.

Knowledge of regression analysis methods, including ordinary least squares, generalized linear models, tree-based methods, regularization approaches, model assumptions, and diagnostic procedures for assessing model fit.

Knowledge of data analysis tools and platforms, including analytical software capabilities, automated systems analysis tools, computational environments for analysis, and the trade-offs between different analytical platform architectures.

Knowledge of data visualization tools and techniques, including chart type selection, visual encoding principles, cognitive load management, accessibility requirements, and the distinction between exploratory and explanatory visualization.

Knowledge of database systems and query languages, including relational database management system principles, SQL capabilities, database theory, query optimization, and the role of database architecture in analytical performance.

Knowledge of data collection and gathering methods, including data sourcing techniques, data monitoring tools, systematic data acquisition approaches, and the assessment of data availability and fitness for analytical use.

Knowledge of data aggregation and correlation techniques, including methods for combining data from multiple sources, identifying statistical relationships, event correlation analysis, and cross-dataset integration approaches.

Knowledge of data processing and transformation principles, including data manipulation techniques, data mapping methods, format conversion, cleansing procedures, and the impact of transformation choices on analytical outcomes.

Knowledge of data classification and profiling methods, including data type taxonomies, data characteristics assessment, completeness and consistency measurement, and the use of profiling to determine data fitness for analysis.

Knowledge of anomaly detection and continuous monitoring methods, including statistical process control, automated alerting threshold design, change point detection, and the application of monitoring systems to operational analytics.

Knowledge of root cause and diagnostic analysis techniques, including fault identification methods, causal investigation approaches, and systematic diagnostic procedures for determining the underlying drivers of observed analytical patterns.

Knowledge of analytical risk quantification, including risk assessment tools and techniques, methods for translating risk into measurable metrics, and the application of quantitative analysis to organizational risk evaluation.

Knowledge of testing and validation methods for analytical outputs, including result verification techniques, evaluation and validation requirements, independent testing approaches, and quality assurance processes for analytical deliverables.

Knowledge of analytic frameworks and structured analysis methods, including established analytical constructs, standards for analytical practice, and the selection and application of structured approaches to complex business problems.

Knowledge of performance measurement and KPI design, including leading and lagging indicators, metric decomposition, measures of effectiveness and performance, and the governance of metric definitions across business units.

Knowledge of reporting and information management practices, including reporting standards and procedures, information management principles, content lifecycle management, and the design of systematic reporting processes.

Knowledge of research methodology and multi-source synthesis, including methods for gathering, integrating, and summarizing information from diverse sources, and techniques for producing coherent analytical findings from heterogeneous data.

Knowledge of simulation and scenario analysis methods, including simulation testing tools, scenario modeling techniques, what-if analysis approaches, and the use of simulation to predict and measure outcomes under uncertainty.

Knowledge of business modeling and analytical requirements translation, including the conversion of business questions into structured analytical approaches, capabilities and requirements analysis, and the alignment of analytical methods to business context.

Knowledge of data model design principles, including conceptual, logical, and physical data models, dimensional modeling, star and snowflake schema design, and the impact of data model choices on analytical query performance.

Knowledge of algorithms and computational methods for analytics, including algorithm selection, computational complexity considerations, and the application of algorithmic approaches to analytical problem solving.

Knowledge of machine learning fundamentals as applied to analytics, including supervised and unsupervised learning concepts, predictive model selection, and the integration of machine learning techniques into analytical workflows.

Knowledge of privacy-preserving analytical techniques, including data de-identification methods, anonymization approaches, and the application of privacy-protective measures that maintain analytical utility while meeting data protection requirements.

Knowledge of data asset management and valuation for analytics, including data inventory and catalog practices, data provenance and lineage tracking, data fitness-for-purpose assessment at the organizational level, and the principles of managing data as a strategic asset to maximize analytical value.
